research_question: Do simple systematic alpha signals survive costs, out-of-sample testing, robustness checks, and market stress?
headline: Testing whether systematic alpha signals survive costs, out-of-sample validation, and market stress.
verdict_headline: Of five signals, 3 survived, 1 was conditional, and 1 was rejected — discipline, not a single best backtest, separates them.
data_note: Dividend- and split-adjusted close for 15 ETFs, 2006-01-03 to 2026-06-18. The cost-aware backtest runs from 2007-02 once a 12-month signal history exists, and each ETF enters on its own inception date.
